title: Red Hat Security Advisory: tar security update
overview:
An update for tar is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 10.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Moderate. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

full_text:
Red Hat Security Advisory: tar security update

An update for tar is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 10.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Moderate. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

An update for tar is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 10.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Moderate. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

Updated Packages:
tar-1.35-9.el10_1.src.rpm
tar-1.35-9.el10_1.x86_64.rpm
tar-debuginfo-1.35-9.el10_1.x86_64.rpm
tar-debugsource-1.35-9.el10_1.x86_64.rpm
tar-1.35-9.el10_1.s390x.rpm
tar-debuginfo-1.35-9.el10_1.s390x.rpm
tar-debugsource-1.35-9.el10_1.s390x.rpm
tar-1.35-9.el10_1.ppc64le.rpm
tar-debuginfo-1.35-9.el10_1.ppc64le.rpm
tar-debugsource-1.35-9.el10_1.ppc64le.rpm
tar-1.35-9.el10_1.aarch64.rpm
tar-debuginfo-1.35-9.el10_1.aarch64.rpm
tar-debugsource-1.35-9.el10_1.aarch64.rpm

CVE-2025-45582: A relative path traversal flaw was found in the gnu tar utility. When archives with relative paths are extracted without the ‘--keep-old-files’ (‘-k’), the extraction process may overwrite existing files that the current user has access to. The server may be impacted if these files are critical to the operation of some service.
